pr_title: Fix: Improve error handling but break transaction

agent_summaries.performance: Performance analysis: Found 2 issues (1 high, 1 medium)
agent_summaries.architecture: Architecture analysis: Found 4 issues (2 medium, 2 low)

priority_issues.description: The create_user function is vulnerable to SQL injection due to direct concatenation of user inputs into the SQL query.
priority_issues.recommendation: Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection attacks. Replace direct string concatenation with parameter placeholders.

priority_issues.description: Potential N+1 query issue in user creation
priority_issues.recommendation: Batch insert multiple users at once to reduce database round trips

priority_issues.description: Potential inefficiency in user input validation
priority_issues.action: Fix soon, can merge with tracking issue

priority_issues.description: The create_user method lacks proper unit tests for the error handling logic.
priority_issues.recommendation: Write unit tests to cover different scenarios for user input validation and error handling.
priority_issues.action: Fix soon, can merge with tracking issue

priority_issues.description: The create_user method violates the Single Responsibility Principle by handling both user input validation and database interaction in the same method.
priority_issues.recommendation: Refactor the create_user method to separate user input validation into a separate method/class.
priority_issues.action: Fix soon, can merge with tracking issue

priority_issues.description: The create_user method is becoming lengthy and could lead to maintenance issues in the future.
priority_issues.recommendation: Extract the user input validation logic into a separate method to reduce the length of create_user.

priority_issues.description: The create_user method shows a hint of feature envy by directly accessing the 'connection' attribute.
priority_issues.recommendation: Consider moving the database interaction logic to a separate class to reduce feature envy.
